maxContractTerm: No statutory maximum found. Hawaii is a single statewide school district (the Hawaii State Department of Education, "HIDOE"), and there is no transportation-specific statute capping the contract term for a school-bus or pupil-transportation contract between the State and a private contractor. The school-bus-specific statute, HRS section 302A-407 ("School bus contracts"), regulates only safety equipment, signage, and driver/vehicle standards (subsection (a)) and operation of the school-bus fare revolving fund (subsection (b)); it is silent on contract term, renewal, and rebid. Notably, Act 257, Session Laws of Hawaii 2013 (S.B. 1082), affirmatively REPEALED the prior school-bus-specific extension framework in section 302A-407 (which had previously authorized two-year extensions by mutual agreement, with an additional two-year extension, capped at a five-per-cent annual compensation increase plus reasonable fuel-cost adjustments) — the legislature's stated purpose was to give the department of education "greater flexibility in the contracting of student transportation" by routing school-bus contracting through the general procurement framework. Term limits are therefore now governed by Hawaii's general procurement statute (the Hawaii Public Procurement Code, HRS chapter 103D), which applies to HIDOE because HIDOE is a "department ... of the executive ... branch of the State" within the definition of "Governmental body" in HRS section 103D-104, with HRS section 103D-102(a) applying chapter 103D "to all procurement contracts made by governmental bodies." The Governor's August 17, 2023 Proclamation Relating to School Bus Services confirms this in operation by expressly suspending HRS chapter 103D for HIDOE school-bus contracting during the school-bus-driver-shortage emergency — a suspension that would have been unnecessary if chapter 103D did not otherwise govern. The governing term rule is HRS section 103D-315 (Multi-term contracts): "Unless otherwise provided by law, a contract for goods or services may be entered into for any period of time deemed to be in the best interests of the governmental body, provided the term of the contract and conditions of renewal or extension, if any, are included in the solicitation and funds are available for the first fiscal period at the time of contracting. Payment and performance obligations for succeeding fiscal periods shall be subject to the availability and appropriation of funds therefor." The implementing administrative rule, HAR section 3-122-149(e), confirms: "A multi-term contract may be entered into for any period of time deemed to be in the best interest of the State, provided the head of the purchasing agency determines in writing that ... [t]he contract will serve the best interest of the State by encouraging effective competition or otherwise promoting economies in procurement; and ... [s]ufficient funds to pay for the initial term of the contract are available." HAR section 3-122-149(d)(1)(A) expressly identifies "a student bus transportation contractor" as a paradigm use case for multi-term contracts because of "high start-up costs." Because each succeeding fiscal period is subject to the appropriation and availability of funds (HRS section 103D-315(a) and (c); HAR section 3-122-149(b) and (g)) and to the contract's own termination clause, any multi-year HIDOE school-bus contract functions as a multi-year award with annual cancellation rights tied to nonappropriation rather than as a guaranteed multi-year obligation.
rebidRequired: Conditional — competitive sealed bidding is the statutory default at every new award. HRS section 103D-302(a) provides: "Contracts shall be awarded by competitive sealed bidding except as otherwise provided in section 103D-301." HRS section 103D-301 lists the only authorized source-selection methods: competitive sealed bidding (section 103D-302), competitive sealed proposals (section 103D-303), professional-services procurement (section 103D-304), small purchases (section 103D-305), sole-source procurement (section 103D-306), and emergency procurements (section 103D-307). At term-end of an HIDOE school-bus contract, the next "new" award must therefore use one of those statutory methods — most often competitive sealed bidding (IFB) or competitive sealed proposals (RFP). However, no full rebid is required if (i) the existing contract's multi-term provisions for renewal or extension, disclosed in the original solicitation, are exercised within their stated term (HRS section 103D-315(a); HAR section 3-122-149(f)(1)); or (ii) a statutory exemption applies, including sole-source procurement under HRS section 103D-306 (with the sole-source contract period capped at one year under HAR section 3-122-81(d) unless approval for a multi-term sole-source contract is granted under HAR section 3-122-149), emergency procurement under HRS section 103D-307 (used, for example, in the wake of the August 17, 2023 Governor's Proclamation suspending HRS chapter 103D for school-bus driver shortages), small-purchase procedures under HRS section 103D-305, cooperative purchasing under HRS chapter 103D Part VIII (sections 103D-801 et seq.), or a short-term extension of up to 180 calendar days approved by the chief procurement officer under HAR section 3-122-3 (as amended July 10, 2025). Outside those exemptions, the State must run a new competitive procurement at the end of the awarded term.
renewalMechanics: Renewal/extension without a full new competitive procurement is permitted only on the conditions built into the multi-term-contract framework. Under HRS section 103D-315 and HAR section 3-122-149(f), the original solicitation must disclose "[t]he term of the contract and conditions for renewal or extension, if any," together with the fact that funds are available only for the initial fiscal period and the obligation in each succeeding fiscal period is subject to appropriation. Where the solicitation builds in defined renewal options (for example, a base term plus stated renewal-option periods, or a price-adjustment clause), those may be exercised without a new IFB/RFP. HAR section 3-122-149(f)(4) requires the solicitation to state "[t]hat a unit price shall be given for each good or service, and that the unit prices shall be the same throughout the contract, except to the extent price adjustment is allowed" — so CPI, fuel, or other indexed escalators are permitted only if expressly set out in the solicitation and resulting contract. Beyond contract-built-in renewals, two narrow extra-contractual mechanisms exist: (1) HAR section 3-122-3 ("Extension of time on contracts"), as amended July 10, 2025, allows the chief procurement officer to extend a contract that has exhausted (or lacks) extension provisions for one or more periods of "one hundred eighty calendar days or less," on a written impracticability determination, party agreement, and same-or-fair-and-reasonable pricing; and (2) HRS section 103D-309 makes any contract awarded pursuant to HRS section 103D-302, 103D-303, or 103D-306 nonbinding without the requisite appropriations certificate, and HRS section 103D-315(a)/(c) and HAR section 3-122-149(b)/(g) make each succeeding fiscal period of a multi-term contract contingent on appropriation, with cancellation triggering reimbursement of the contractor's reasonable, nonamortized, nonrecurring costs. Automatic evergreen renewal without a chief-procurement-officer determination or solicitation-disclosed renewal option is not authorized. (Note: the prior school-bus-specific extension rule that allowed two-year mutual-agreement extensions with a five-per-cent annual compensation cap and reasonable fuel adjustments was REPEALED by Act 257, SLH 2013, and has no current legal force.)
statutoryCitation: Primary contract-term/rebid/renewal authority: Hawaii Revised Statutes section 103D-315 (Multi-term contracts) (no statutory maximum term; renewal conditions must be stated in the solicitation; succeeding fiscal-period obligations subject to appropriation), section 103D-301 (Methods of source selection), section 103D-302 (Competitive sealed bidding), section 103D-309 (Contract not binding unless funds available), section 103D-306 (Sole source procurement), and section 103D-307 (Emergency procurements) — official text in the Hawaii State Legislature publication of HRS Chapter 103D, https://data.capitol.hawaii.gov/sessions/session2017/HRS-Chapter-PDF's/HRS_0103D.pdf (re-verified verbatim 2026-06-23). Applicability of HRS chapter 103D to HIDOE: HRS section 103D-102 (Application of this chapter — to "all procurement contracts made by governmental bodies") and HRS section 103D-104 (Definitions — "Governmental body" includes "any department ... of the executive ... branch of the State"). Implementing administrative rule on multi-term contracts (and explicit "student bus transportation contractor" example): Hawaii Administrative Rules section 3-122-149 (State Procurement Office official PDF at https://spo.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2013/11/3-122.pdf) — re-verified verbatim 2026-06-23. Sole-source one-year cap: HAR section 3-122-81(d) (same PDF). Extension of time: HAR section 3-122-3 (as amended July 10, 2025), official SPO PDF at https://spo.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/RAM-HAR-3-122-Subchapters-2-to-5_am_PPB-revised-7-10-2025.pdf. School-bus-contract-specific safety statute (silent on term/rebid/renewal): HRS section 302A-407 (School bus contracts), and DOE transportation authority HRS section 302A-406 (Transportation of school children) — official text in HRS Chapter 302A PDF at https://data.capitol.hawaii.gov/sessions/session2017/HRS-Chapter-PDF's/HRS_0302A.pdf at sections 302A-406, 302A-407 (last amended L 2013, c 257). Statute history for 302A-407: Act 257 of 2013 (S.B. 1082) official text at https://data.capitol.hawaii.gov/sessions/sessionlaws/Years/SLH2013/SLH2013_Act257.pdf — re-verified verbatim 2026-06-23 (Act 257 affirmatively repealed the prior two-year-extension framework). Confirmation that HRS chapter 103D governs HIDOE school-bus contracting: Governor's Proclamation Relating to School Bus Services (Aug. 17, 2023) at https://dod.hawaii.gov/hiema/files/2023/01/2023-08-17-Proclamation-Relating-to-School-Bus-Services.pdf — re-verified verbatim 2026-06-23.
districtVariance: No — Hawaii is unique among U.S. states in having a single statewide school district. The Hawaii State Department of Education (HIDOE) is the sole local educational agency for public, non-charter schools statewide, and all school-bus procurement is administered centrally through HIDOE's Procurement and Contracts Branch under the State Procurement Office's chief-procurement-officer authority and HRS chapter 103D. There are therefore no separate county or local "school districts" that could interpret HRS chapter 103D or HRS section 302A-407 differently from one another for student-transportation contracting. The only adjacent governance structure is the State Public Charter School Commission and the individual public charter schools governed under HRS chapter 302D — but those entities operate under a SEPARATE statutory framework, are statutorily EXEMPT from HRS chapter 103D (with their governing boards required instead to adopt their own internal procurement policies consistent with public-accountability goals), and do not procure HIDOE-administered school-bus routes. Thus there is no district-level variance affecting HIDOE-administered student-transportation procurement.
specialEducation: No separately codified term-limit, rebid, renewal, or procurement rule was located for special-education, IEP-mandated, McKinney-Vento, or foster-care student transportation contracts in Hawaii. HRS section 302A-406 (Transportation of school children) authorizes HIDOE to "provide suitable transportation to and from school and for educational field trips for all children in grades kindergarten to twelve and in special education classes" — but the same statute, not a separate provision, governs both regular and special-education transportation, and HIDOE's procurement of vendors serving students with disabilities is conducted under the same HRS chapter 103D / HAR Title 3, Chapter 122 framework as regular-route contracting (i.e., HRS section 103D-302 competitive sealed bidding by default; HRS section 103D-315 / HAR section 3-122-149 governing multi-term contracts; HRS section 103D-306 / HAR section 3-122-81 sole-source procurement available where only one vendor can serve a particular IEP need; HRS section 103D-307 emergency procurement). HIDOE administrative guidance (e.g., Form ST-11 for curb-to-curb transportation and Form ST-16 for IEP-approved special-education transportation, and HAR Title 8, Chapter 60 governing FAPE for students with disabilities) routes IEP-related transportation operations through HIDOE's regular procurement framework rather than carving out separate contracting rules. Federal law (the Individuals with Disabilities Education Act, 20 U.S.C. section 1400 et seq., and the McKinney-Vento Homeless Assistance Act, 42 U.S.C. section 11431 et seq.) requires HIDOE to provide transportation as part of a free appropriate public education or for homeless students, but those federal duties do not alter Hawaii's state contracting/procurement rules — they govern the duty to transport, not the term, rebid, or renewal mechanics of the procurement instrument. No Hawaii statute or administrative rule located in this research carves out special-education student-transportation contracts from the HRS chapter 103D / HAR 3-122 procurement framework.
notesAndSpecialProvisions:
- **Single statewide LEA.** Hawaii is the only U.S. state with one statewide school district. All public-school student-transportation contracting is procured by the Hawaii State Department of Education ("HIDOE") and its Procurement and Contracts Branch, operating under the State Procurement Office. There is no county-by-county or board-by-board procurement variation.

- **Procurement code applies to HIDOE.** HRS section 103D-102(a) applies HRS chapter 103D to "all procurement contracts made by governmental bodies," and HRS section 103D-104 defines "Governmental body" to include "any department, commission, council, board, bureau, authority, committee, institution, legislative body, agency, government corporation, or other establishment or office of the executive, legislative, or judicial branch of the State, including the office of Hawaiian affairs, and the several counties of the State." HIDOE is a state executive department. The Governor's August 17, 2023 Proclamation Relating to School Bus Services expressly suspends HRS chapter 103D for HIDOE school-bus contracting during the school-bus-driver-shortage emergency, which presupposes that chapter 103D otherwise applies.

- **Prior school-bus-specific extension framework REPEALED in 2013.** Before Act 257, SLH 2013 (S.B. 1082), HRS section 302A-407 contained subsections expressly authorizing two-year extensions of school-bus contracts by mutual agreement (with an additional two-year extension), capped at a five-per-cent annual compensation increase plus reasonable fuel-cost adjustments, plus renegotiation procedures and a vehicle-age limit and refurbishment requirement. Act 257 (effective July 2, 2013) struck all of that framework, leaving 302A-407 with only safety-equipment/signage/standards rules (subsection (a)) and the school-bus fare revolving fund (subsection (b)). The legislative purpose recital in Act 257 expressly justified the change as giving DOE "greater flexibility in the contracting of student transportation" by routing school-bus contracting through HRS chapter 103D. Any older secondary source still describing two-year mutual extensions or 5% compensation caps as part of 302A-407 is reporting REPEALED law.

- **No current transportation-specific term cap.** As amended, HRS section 302A-407 is the only school-bus-contract-specific statute, and it does not address contract term, competitive procurement, renewal, or rebid. The contracting framework therefore defaults entirely to HRS chapter 103D.

- **Multi-term contracts explicitly contemplate school-bus contractors.** HAR section 3-122-149(d)(1)(A) gives, as a paradigm example of when a multi-term contract is appropriate, "a student bus transportation contractor would incur high start-up costs for buses and other equipment solely to meet the State's requirements but the equipment would have useful life in excess of one year."

- **Mandatory non-appropriation clause.** Every HIDOE multi-term school-bus contract is, by force of HRS section 103D-309 and HRS section 103D-315(a)/(c) and HAR section 3-122-149(b)/(c)/(g), subject to cancellation if funds are not appropriated for a succeeding fiscal period; upon such cancellation the contractor is reimbursed for reasonable nonamortized, nonrecurring costs.

- **Default source-selection method = competitive sealed bidding.** Under HRS section 103D-302(a) every new HIDOE school-bus contract award must be by competitive sealed bidding "except as otherwise provided in section 103D-301" (i.e., except via RFP, professional services, small purchase, sole source, or emergency procurement).

- **Sole-source one-year ceiling (CORRECTED CITATION).** HAR section 3-122-81(d) provides: "The contract period for a sole source procurement shall not exceed one year, unless approval is granted for a multi-term contract pursuant to section 3-122-149." (Stage-1 cited HAR 3-122-66.5; that section does not exist as a sole-source cap — HAR 3-122-66 was a distinct "Waiver to requirement for procurement of professional services" rule that has been repealed. The substantive one-year cap rule is correctly located in HAR 3-122-81(d).)

- **Short extension authority.** HAR section 3-122-3 (as amended July 10, 2025) allows the chief procurement officer to extend a contract — including one that has exhausted its built-in extension provisions — for periods of "one hundred eighty calendar days or less" each, on a written impracticability determination, party agreement, and same-or-fair-and-reasonable pricing.

- **Cooperative/piggyback purchasing.** HRS chapter 103D, Part VIII (sections 103D-801 et seq.) authorizes cooperative purchasing among public procurement units, providing an alternative to a full new IFB/RFP where applicable.

- **Emergency procurement.** HRS section 103D-307 (Emergency procurements) was effectively invoked statewide for school-bus services when the Governor's August 17, 2023 Proclamation suspended HRS chapter 103D for HIDOE school-bus driver-shortage contracting.

- **2026 session — passed but not yet act-numbered as of as-of date.** Two 2026 measures had passed both chambers and been transmitted to the Governor as of May 2026 (as of date 2026-06-23): (i) SB 2075 (RELATING TO PUBLIC PROCUREMENT) amends HRS 103D-302 and 103D-303 to add a 5% Hawaii bidder/offeror preference for evaluation purposes (capped at $500,000) and adds debarment causes; this does NOT change contract-term/rebid/renewal mechanics. (ii) HB 1785 (RELATING TO STUDENT TRANSPORTATION) adds a new HRS 302A section allowing the superintendent to temporarily suspend or remove a service segment if a student-transportation contractor fails to provide service for 5 or more consecutive instructional days and to enter an interim agreement with another qualified carrier, requiring DOE to complete "a competitive procurement under Hawaii public procurement code law for a permanent replacement service within 12 months" — confirming, not displacing, the HRS 103D framework. Neither measure has been confirmed as signed into law (Act number) by the as-of date.

- **Charter schools.** Hawaii charter schools operate under HRS chapter 302D and are STATUTORILY EXEMPT from HRS chapter 103D; they must develop internal procurement policies consistent with public-accountability goals. Their transportation procurement is not governed by HIDOE's processes. Charter schools are an explicit out-scope statutory carve-out from HIDOE-administered school-transportation contracts.

- **Special-education transportation.** HRS section 302A-406 authorizes transportation "for all children in grades kindergarten to twelve and in special education classes," but no separate Hawaii statute or HAR provision sets distinct term, rebid, renewal, or procurement rules for special-education, IEP, McKinney-Vento, foster-care, or alternative transportation. Federal IDEA/McKinney-Vento duties govern the obligation to transport but do not displace HRS chapter 103D's contracting rules.

- **No board approval requirement codified.** Nothing in HRS section 302A-407 or HRS chapter 103D requires Board of Education approval of an individual school-bus contract or its renewal; procurement decisions are made by the chief procurement officer or the head of the purchasing agency (HIDOE).
exceptionsUnresolved: All five fields resolved on primary authority. One minor citation in the Stage-1 Notes (HAR 3-122-66.5) was traced to a nonexistent or unrelated rule and replaced with the correct rule (HAR 3-122-81(d)); the substantive sole-source one-year-cap claim is unchanged. Two 2026 measures (SB 2075 — Hawaii bidder preference for procurement; HB 1785 — student-transportation contractor non-performance) had passed both chambers and were transmitted to the Governor as of May 2026 but their final Act numbers and signed status are not visible in the LRB 2026 acts list as of the 2026-06-23 as-of date; neither measure alters the substantive term/rebid/renewal findings as drafted, and both explicitly route DOE bus procurement through HRS chapter 103D.

Payment and performance obligations for succeeding fiscal periods shall be subject to the availability and appropriation of funds therefor."

sourceLedgerRows.citation: Haw. Rev. Stat. section 103D-102 (Application of this chapter); section 103D-104 (Definitions — "Governmental body")
sourceLedgerRows.codeCurrency: Hawaii State Legislature publication; section 103D-102 last amended L 2014, c 96, section 13; section 103D-104 last amended L 2011, c 211, section 2. Re-verified verbatim 2026-06-23.
sourceLedgerRows.fieldsSupported: All five fields (establishes that HRS chapter 103D governs HIDOE)
sourceLedgerRows.relevantText: Quoted (103D-102(a)): "This chapter shall apply to all procurement contracts made by governmental bodies whether the consideration for the contract is cash, revenues, realizations, receipts, or earnings, any of which the State receives or is owed; in-kind benefits; or forbearance ..." Quoted (103D-104, definition of Governmental body): "any department, commission, council, board, bureau, authority, committee, institution, legislative body, agency, government corporation, or other establishment or office of the executive, legislative, or judicial branch of the State, including the office of Hawaiian affairs, and the several counties of the State."

sourceLedgerRows.citation: Haw. Rev. Stat. section 302A-406 (Transportation of school children); section 302A-407 (School bus contracts), in HRS Chapter 302A PDF published by the Hawaii State Legislature
sourceLedgerRows.codeCurrency: Hawaii State Legislature publication; section 302A-406 credit line: "[L 1996, c 89, pt of section 2; am L 2010, c 167, section 5; am L 2013, c 257, section 2]"; section 302A-407 credit line: "[L 1996, c 89, pt of section 2; am L 2002, c 108, section 3; am L 2013, c 257, section 3]." Re-verified verbatim 2026-06-23. No subsequent amendments to either section located in 2022, 2023, 2024, or 2025 LRB Acts; the 2026 session's HB 1785 (RELATING TO STUDENT TRANSPORTATION) was passed by both chambers and transmitted to the Governor May 7, 2026 but adds a NEW section to HRS chapter 302A on contractor non-performance and amends HRS 302A-407.5 (revolving fund); it does NOT amend section 302A-407.
sourceLedgerRows.fieldsSupported: All five fields (establishes silence of school-bus-specific statute on term/rebid/renewal)
sourceLedgerRows.relevantText: Quoted (302A-407(a) full text): "Any school bus contract between the State and the contractor shall include a provision requiring the contractor to equip the contractor's vehicles with the signs and visual signals described in section 291C-95(d) and (g). The contract shall also include other provisions as may be deemed necessary by the State for the safety of school bus passengers and shall include provisions requiring compliance with the rules and standards described in section 286-181." Quoted (302A-407(b)): operates the school-bus fare revolving fund. Statute addresses only safety equipment, signage, and standards — silent on term/competitive selection/renewal.

sourceLedgerRows.citation: Act 257, Session Laws of Hawaii 2013 (S.B. 1082) — "A Bill for an Act Relating to Transportation of School Children"

sourceLedgerRows.fieldsSupported: Max Contract Term (Field 1); Renewal Mechanics (Field 3); Notes (historical extension framework)
sourceLedgerRows.relevantText: Act 257 expressly amends section 302A-407 by REPEALING the prior subsections that had authorized two-year mutual-agreement extensions, an additional two-year extension, a five-per-cent annual compensation cap, reasonable fuel-cost adjustments, post-disruption renegotiation, and a vehicle-age/refurbishment regime — leaving only the current safety-equipment subsection (a) and revolving-fund subsection (b). The Act's purpose recital: "to provide the department of education with greater flexibility in the contracting of student transportation to improve service delivery and contain escalating costs."

sourceLedgerRows.codeCurrency: State Procurement Office official PDF; credit line on section 3-122-81: "[Eff 12/15/95; am and comp 11/17/97; am 7/6/99; am and comp MAR 21 2008]". Re-verified verbatim 2026-06-23.

sourceLedgerRows.citation: Haw. Code R. (HAR) section 3-122-3 (Extension of time on contracts), as amended by the Procurement Policy Board July 10, 2025
sourceLedgerRows.codeCurrency: Filed amendment effective July 10, 2025; published by State Procurement Office. Re-verified verbatim 2026-06-23.
sourceLedgerRows.relevantText: Quoted (3-122-3(a)): "If a contract has exhausted its provision for extension(s) of time of performance, or if the contract does not include a provision for extension(s) of time of performance, the contract may be extended upon approval of the chief procurement officer, provided: (1) The period of each extension is for one hundred eighty calendar days or less; (2) The procurement officer makes a written determination that it is not practical to award another contract at the time of the expiration of the contract for reasons to include but not be limited to the following: (A) A new contract cannot be executed by the time the contract expires; or (B) The need for the good or service is short term; (3) All parties agree to the extension of time of performance; and (4) The price(s) or conditions of the contract remain the same as the original contract, or as amended per the contract; or if not the same or as amended, they are fair and reasonable."

sourceLedgerRows.sourceTier: 5 — Currentness-check (negative result; passed but not yet act-numbered)
sourceLedgerRows.citation: Hawaii Legislative Reference Bureau, "Bills Passed by the Hawaii State Legislature, Regular Session of 2026" (as of May 9, 2026): HB 1785 HD1 SD1 CD1 (RELATING TO STUDENT TRANSPORTATION) and SB 2075 SD1 HD2 CD1 (RELATING TO PUBLIC PROCUREMENT)
sourceLedgerRows.codeCurrency: LRB 2026 passed-bills list as of May 9, 2026; both measures "Received by the Governor" (HB 1785 May 7, SB 2075 May 8). Neither appears with a final Act number as of as-of date 2026-06-23.
sourceLedgerRows.relevantText: HB 1785: "Requires the department to complete a competitive procurement under Hawaii public procurement code law for a permanent replacement service within 12 months of executing an interim agreement under this provision." Section Affected: 302A- (1 SECTION), 302A-407.5. SB 2075: amends Hawaii bidder preference for competitive sealed bidding and competitive sealed proposals; Section Affected: 103D-302, 103D-303, 103D-702, 103D-1001, 103D-1002. Neither alters contract-term/rebid/renewal mechanics.

challengeMatrixRows.findingChallenged: Max Contract Term: No statutory maximum found; HRS chapter 103D-315 governs.
challengeMatrixRows.disconfirmingHypothesis: Hypothesis: a transportation-specific or education-specific term cap exists, either in HRS 302A (Education) or in HAR Title 8 (Department of Education), that overrides HRS 103D-315 — OR a school-bus-specific extension/renewal-option framework still in force (e.g., a two-year-extension/5% compensation cap).
challengeMatrixRows.challengeSearches: Re-read entire HRS Chapter 302A PDF for any 302A-40x provision touching term/duration/renewal/extension/years/not-exceed/multi-year/notwithstanding. Searched LRB 2022, 2023, 2024, 2025 Acts and the 2026 passed-bills list for any amendment to 302A-407, 302A-406, or 103D-315. Pulled Act 257, SLH 2013 (SB 1082) to confirm what the legislature actually did to 302A-407. Investigated the prominent secondary-source assertion (Google snippet) that 302A-407 currently authorizes 'two years by mutual agreement ... five per cent ... fuel'. Tested whether SB 2803 (2022, zero-emission bus 2045 contract cap) was enacted.
challengeMatrixRows.result: CONFIRMED. (1) HRS 302A contains no current term/renewal/extension framework for school-bus contracts; 302A-407 addresses only safety equipment and the revolving fund (subsection (a)) and (b)). (2) Act 257 SLH 2013 (S.B. 1082) AFFIRMATIVELY REPEALED the pre-2013 two-year-extension/5%-cap/fuel-adjustment framework — secondary sources still describing those provisions as current are reporting repealed law. (3) SB 2803 (2022, zero-emission bus contract-term restriction past January 1, 2045) does NOT appear in the LRB 2022 Acts list — it was NOT enacted, so there is no current zero-emission-bus contract-term constraint on 302A-407 either. (4) No 2023-2026 amendment to 103D-315 or 302A-407 located.
challengeMatrixRows.confidenceImpact: Confidence held at High; the adversarial pass added a definitive currentness anchor (Act 257 SLH 2013) and ruled out two prominent disconfirming hypotheses (the supposed two-year-extension/5% provision; the supposed zero-emission contract-term provision).

challengeMatrixRows.findingChallenged: Rebid Required: Conditional — competitive sealed bidding is the statutory default; exemptions exist.
challengeMatrixRows.disconfirmingHypothesis: Hypothesis: a mandatory full-rebid rule applies that would override 103D-302(a)'s 'except as otherwise provided in section 103D-301' carve-outs — OR an extra renewal-without-rebid pathway exists (board waiver, agency approval) that Stage 1 missed.
challengeMatrixRows.challengeSearches: Reviewed HRS 103D-301 (Methods of source selection) for the full menu of source-selection statutes; reviewed HRS 103D-302, 103D-305, 103D-306, 103D-307 for full text; reviewed HAR 3-122-3, 3-122-81, 3-122-149 for renewal/extension limits; reviewed Part VIII (103D-801 et seq.) for cooperative purchasing; checked 302A-407 for any rebid mandate. Searched LRB 2022-2026 Acts for any change to the competitive-bidding default.
challengeMatrixRows.result: CONFIRMED. HRS 103D-302(a) imposes a competitive-sealed-bidding default; the statutory exemption list in 103D-301 is closed (CSB, CSP, professional services, small purchases, sole source, emergency procurement); HAR 3-122-3 adds the 180-day chief-procurement-officer extension; cooperative purchasing under 103D-801 et seq. is available. No mandatory full-rebid override located; no extra board-waiver or agency-approval renewal pathway located. The Governor's 2023 Proclamation demonstrated that even the chapter-wide suspension required a formal Proclamation.

challengeMatrixRows.findingChallenged: Renewal Mechanics: HRS 103D-315 + HAR 3-122-149(f) (solicitation-disclosed renewals) and HAR 3-122-3 (180-day CPO extension); HRS 103D-309 appropriation contingency.
challengeMatrixRows.disconfirmingHypothesis: Hypothesis: a CPI cap, fuel-adjustment cap, public-hearing requirement, or maximum-number-of-renewals limit on multi-term renewals applies that Stage 1 missed — OR an automatic evergreen-renewal authority exists.
challengeMatrixRows.challengeSearches: Re-read HAR 3-122-149(f) full text for any limit on renewal pricing or number of renewals; re-read HAR 3-122-3 full text for any restriction beyond the 180-day-per-extension cap; reviewed HRS 103D-315(b)-(c) for any pre-renewal written-determination requirement; searched Stage-1's claim that pre-2013 fuel/CPI caps survived (they did not — they were repealed in Act 257 SLH 2013).
challengeMatrixRows.result: CONFIRMED. HAR 3-122-149(f)(4) requires unit prices to be the same throughout the contract "except to the extent price adjustment is allowed," placing CPI/fuel/index escalators inside the solicitation's defined boundaries; HAR 3-122-3 caps each extension at 180 days and requires written impracticability + party agreement + same-or-fair-and-reasonable pricing; no public-hearing requirement located; no maximum-renewals number located; no automatic evergreen-renewal authority located.

challengeMatrixRows.disconfirmingHypothesis: Hypothesis: one or more cited sections does not say what Stage 1 claimed, or has been renumbered/repealed/superseded — most pressing test: the Stage-1 citation to HAR 3-122-66.5 for the sole-source one-year cap.
challengeMatrixRows.challengeSearches: Re-opened all cited PDFs (HRS 103D PDF; HRS 302A PDF; HAR 3-122 PDF; HAR 3-122-3 July 10, 2025 amendment PDF; Governor's August 17, 2023 Proclamation PDF; SLH 2013 Act 257 PDF; LRB 2022-2026 Acts/Passed lists) and compared verbatim. Specifically searched HAR 3-122 PDF for 3-122-66.5 (none found) and verified that HAR 3-122-66 is a 'Waiver to requirement for procurement of professional services' rule (unrelated to sole-source caps) and has been repealed.
challengeMatrixRows.result: PARTIALLY REFUTED — corrected. All eleven primary statutory citations re-verified verbatim from official PDFs. ONE Notes-section rule citation refuted: Stage 1's HAR 3-122-66.5 is not the source of the sole-source one-year cap; the correct rule is HAR 3-122-81(d) ("The contract period for a sole source procurement shall not exceed one year, unless approval is granted for a multi-term contract pursuant to section 3-122-149"). The substantive sole-source one-year-cap claim is unchanged; only the rule number is corrected.
challengeMatrixRows.confidenceImpact: Confidence held at High on the primary citation chain (load-bearing for Fields 1-3 are all verbatim-confirmed); Notes-section citation corrected.

challengeMatrixRows.findingChallenged: Special Education: No separately codified term/rebid/renewal/procurement rule for special-ed transportation; HRS 103D / HAR 3-122 framework applies.
challengeMatrixRows.disconfirmingHypothesis: Hypothesis: a HAR Title 8 (Department of Education) provision, a HRS 302A subsection, an HRS 302D charter-school provision, or an IDEA/McKinney-Vento implementing regulation sets distinct procurement/term/rebid/renewal rules for special-education student-transportation contracts.
challengeMatrixRows.challengeSearches: Searched HRS 302A for special-education-specific procurement provisions; reviewed HIDOE special-education guidance (Form ST-11, Form ST-16, HAR Title 8 Chapter 60 governing FAPE for students with disabilities); searched HRS 302D and 302A-491 et seq. (special-education-related transfer-of-rights, accredited private special-education school provisions); confirmed federal IDEA/McKinney-Vento duties govern the duty to transport, not the term/rebid/renewal of state procurement.
challengeMatrixRows.result: CONFIRMED. No separate Hawaii statute or HAR provision was located that sets distinct procurement/term/rebid/renewal mechanics for special-education, IEP, McKinney-Vento, foster-care, or alternative student-transportation contracts. HIDOE special-education transportation operates within the same HRS 103D / HAR 3-122 framework. Federal IDEA/McKinney-Vento creates duties to transport but does not displace state contracting mechanics.

challengeMatrixRows.findingChallenged: District Variance: No — Hawaii is a single statewide LEA; HIDOE administers all public-school student-transportation contracting centrally.
challengeMatrixRows.disconfirmingHypothesis: Hypothesis: county-level or complex-area variation in HIDOE procurement practice exists — OR charter schools (HRS 302D), which are NOT part of HIDOE, generate procurement variation that should be characterized as 'variance' rather than out-of-scope.
challengeMatrixRows.challengeSearches: Reviewed HIDOE Procurement and Contracts Branch / hands.ehawaii.gov procurement portal; reviewed HIDOE 'Vendors Working with HIDOE' page; reviewed State Procurement Office HRS reference page; reviewed HRS chapter 302D (Public Charter Schools) and the chapter-103D-exemption provision for charter-school governing boards.
challengeMatrixRows.result: CONFIRMED. HIDOE is the sole non-charter public LEA; all bus-route procurement is centralized through the Procurement and Contracts Branch under HRS chapter 103D; there is no county/complex-area procurement variation. Charter schools operate under a SEPARATE statutory framework (HRS 302D) and are statutorily EXEMPT from HRS chapter 103D — but charter schools do not administer HIDOE bus routes, so they are correctly characterized as an out-of-scope statutory carve-out rather than 'variance' within HIDOE procurement.
challengeMatrixRows.confidenceImpact: Confidence held at High; clarification added that charter schools are exempt from HRS chapter 103D, but do not create variance within HIDOE-administered school-bus contracting.

challengeMatrixRows.findingChallenged: Currentness (cross-cutting): All cited sections are current as of as-of date 2026-06-23.
challengeMatrixRows.disconfirmingHypothesis: Hypothesis: 103D-315, 103D-302, 302A-407, or HAR 3-122-149 was amended, repealed, renumbered, or superseded after their stated code-currency dates, including by any 2024, 2025, or 2026 act.
challengeMatrixRows.challengeSearches: Pulled the LRB 2022, 2023, 2024, 2025 enacted-acts lists and the 2026 passed-bills list (as of May 9, 2026); searched each for 302A-407, 302A-406, 103D-315, 103D-302, 103D-301, school bus, pupil/student transportation, procurement; tested the prominent secondary-source claim that SB 2803 (2022, zero-emission bus 2045 contract cap) was enacted.
challengeMatrixRows.result: CONFIRMED current. No 2022-2025 act amends 103D-315, 103D-302, 302A-407 in a way that alters the substantive findings. SB 2803 (2022) was NOT enacted. Two 2026 measures passed both chambers and were transmitted to the Governor as of May 2026 but do not have final Act numbers in the LRB 2026 acts list as of as-of date 2026-06-23: SB 2075 (Hawaii bidder preference for competitive sealed bidding — does not alter term/rebid/renewal mechanics) and HB 1785 (student-transportation contractor non-performance — adds a NEW HRS 302A section and amends HRS 302A-407.5 revolving fund; expressly requires "a competitive procurement under Hawaii public procurement code law" for permanent replacement, confirming the HRS 103D framework). HAR 3-122-3 was amended July 10, 2025 (already reflected); HAR 3-122-149 is unchanged.
challengeMatrixRows.confidenceImpact: Confidence held at High; transmitted-but-not-yet-act-numbered 2026 measures noted in Notes and Exceptions but do not alter substantive findings.

challengeMatrixRows.findingChallenged: Applicability boundary: HRS chapter 103D actually governs HIDOE school-bus contracting (Stage 1 reasoned partly from the Governor's 2023 Proclamation).
challengeMatrixRows.disconfirmingHypothesis: Hypothesis: HRS 302A or another carve-out statute removes HIDOE school-bus contracting from HRS chapter 103D, making the chapter inapplicable.
challengeMatrixRows.challengeSearches: Reviewed HRS 103D-102(b)-(c) exclusion list for any school-bus or HIDOE carve-out; reviewed HRS 302A for any opt-out of chapter 103D; reviewed HRS 103D-104 definition of 'Governmental body'; checked SPO HRS reference page; reviewed Act 257 SLH 2013 legislative-finding text.
challengeMatrixRows.result: CONFIRMED. HRS 103D-102 exclusion list does not exclude HIDOE or school-bus contracts; HRS 103D-104's 'Governmental body' definition includes state executive-branch departments (HIDOE qualifies); SPO confirms 103D applies to state departments; Act 257 SLH 2013's legislative purpose was explicitly to route school-bus contracting through HRS chapter 103D's flexibility framework; the 2023 Proclamation suspending HRS chapter 103D for school-bus contracting confirms in operation that the chapter otherwise applies.

perFieldVerdicts.note: All primary citations re-verified verbatim. Disconfirming hypotheses (school-bus-specific 2-year extension/5% cap; SB 2803 2022 zero-emission contract-term restriction) ruled out — both REPEALED (Act 257 SLH 2013) or NOT ENACTED (SB 2803 2022) respectively. No statutory maximum applies.

HRS 103D-302(a) competitive-sealed-bidding default verified verbatim; HRS 103D-301 closed list of source-selection methods verified verbatim. No mandatory full-rebid override; exemptions correctly enumerated.

HRS 103D-315 + HAR 3-122-149(f) + HAR 3-122-3 (as amended July 10, 2025) all re-verified verbatim. No automatic evergreen authority; CPI/fuel/index escalators only as disclosed in solicitation; 180-day-per-extension CPO authority cap confirmed.

Primary citation chain (HRS 103D-315/302/301/309/102/104; HRS 302A-406/407; HAR 3-122-149; HAR 3-122-3; Act 257 SLH 2013; Governor's August 17, 2023 Proclamation) all re-verified verbatim. ONE non-load-bearing Notes-section citation (HAR 3-122-66.5 for sole-source cap) was REFUTED-AND-CORRECTED to HAR 3-122-81(d); substantive claim is unchanged.

Single statewide LEA structure verified through SPO, HIDOE Procurement and Contracts Branch, and HRS chapter 103D's centralized procurement-officer authority; charter schools clarified as a separate statutory carve-out (HRS 302D) rather than within-HIDOE variance.

No separate Hawaii statute or HAR provision sets distinct procurement/term/rebid/renewal rules for special-education student-transportation contracts; HIDOE administrative guidance (Form ST-11, Form ST-16, HAR Title 8 Chapter 60) routes IEP-related transportation through the standard HRS 103D / HAR 3-122 procurement framework.

verificationNotes: SOURCE-VALIDATION REVISIT (load-bearing primary citations for Max Term / Rebid / Renewal): I re-opened each cited official source on 2026-06-23 and confirmed verbatim. (1) HRS section 103D-315 (Multi-term contracts) — re-opened the Hawaii State Legislature PDF at https://data.capitol.hawaii.gov/sessions/session2017/HRS-Chapter-PDF's/HRS_0103D.pdf (downloaded; text-extracted with pdftotext); section 103D-315 text as Stage 1 quoted, credit line shows enactment L Sp 1993, c 8, pt of section 2, with no subsequent amendments. (2) HRS section 103D-302(a) — same PDF; text "Contracts shall be awarded by competitive sealed bidding except as otherwise provided in section 103D-301" verified verbatim; credit line last amended L 1997, c 352, section 23. (3) HRS section 103D-301 closed list of source-selection methods (CSB, CSP, professional services, small purchases, sole source, emergency procurement) verified verbatim; credit line last amended L 2013, c 239, section 2. (4) HRS section 103D-309 (Contract not binding unless funds available) — same PDF; verified verbatim. (5) HRS section 103D-102 / 103D-104 (chapter applies to all governmental-body procurement contracts; HIDOE is an executive-branch department) — same PDF; both verified verbatim, 103D-102 last amended L 2014, c 96, section 13; 103D-104 last amended L 2011, c 211, section 2. (6) HRS section 302A-407 (School bus contracts) — re-opened the Hawaii State Legislature PDF at https://data.capitol.hawaii.gov/sessions/session2017/HRS-Chapter-PDF's/HRS_0302A.pdf; subsections (a) safety equipment and (b) revolving fund verified verbatim, credit line last amended L 2013, c 257, section 3. (7) HAR section 3-122-149 (Multi-term contract) — re-opened https://spo.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2013/11/3-122.pdf; the (d)(1)(A) "student bus transportation contractor" example, (e) head-of-purchasing-agency determinations, and (f) solicitation-disclosure requirements all verified verbatim. (8) HAR section 3-122-3 (Extension of time on contracts, as amended July 10, 2025) — re-opened https://spo.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/RAM-HAR-3-122-Subchapters-2-to-5_am_PPB-revised-7-10-2025.pdf; subsection (a)(1)-(4) verified verbatim including the 180-calendar-day-or-less limit. (9) Governor's August 17, 2023 Proclamation Relating to School Bus Services — re-opened https://dod.hawaii.gov/hiema/files/2023/01/2023-08-17-Proclamation-Relating-to-School-Bus-Services.pdf; Section II Suspension of Laws expressly suspends "Chapter 103D, HRS, Hawaii public procurement code, to the extent that compliance results in any additional delays involved in meeting procurement requirements for selecting contractors in a timely manner to respond to emergency situations" verified verbatim. (10) Act 257, Session Laws of Hawaii 2013 (S.B. 1082) — re-opened https://data.capitol.hawaii.gov/sessions/sessionlaws/Years/SLH2013/SLH2013_Act257.pdf; the verbatim text confirms that Act 257 REPEALED the prior 302A-407 framework that authorized two-year mutual-agreement extensions, an additional two-year extension, a five-per-cent annual compensation cap, fuel-cost adjustments, post-disruption renegotiation, and the vehicle-age/refurbishment regime — leaving only the current safety-equipment subsection (a) and revolving-fund subsection (b). All ten load-bearing primary sources VERIFIED. None unreachable. ONE non-load-bearing Notes citation (HAR 3-122-66.5 for the sole-source one-year cap) was REFUTED — that rule number does not exist as a sole-source cap; the correct rule is HAR 3-122-81(d), which I re-verified verbatim from the same SPO PDF and have substituted in the final pack. CURRENTNESS sweep: pulled and text-extracted the LRB 2022, 2023, 2024, 2025 enacted-acts lists and the 2026 passed-bills list (as of May 9, 2026). No 2022-2025 act amends HRS 103D-315, HRS 302A-407, or HAR 3-122-149 in any way that alters the substantive findings. SB 2803 (2022, zero-emission bus 2045 contract-term restriction) does NOT appear in the 2022 LRB acts list and was NOT enacted (Google snippet summaries describing 302A-407 as currently containing those provisions were hallucinating from the unenacted bill text). Two 2026 measures passed both chambers and were transmitted to the Governor as of May 2026 but were not yet act-numbered in the LRB 2026 acts list as of as-of date 2026-06-23: SB 2075 (RELATING TO PUBLIC PROCUREMENT, amends 103D-302/303 to add Hawaii bidder preference and debarment causes — does not alter term/rebid/renewal mechanics) and HB 1785 (RELATING TO STUDENT TRANSPORTATION, adds a new HRS 302A section and amends 302A-407.5 to authorize the superintendent to suspend or remove a service segment if a contractor fails for 5 or more consecutive instructional days, with required competitive procurement of permanent replacement within 12 months under the Hawaii public procurement code). Both 2026 measures confirm, rather than displace, the HRS 103D framework. ADVERSARIAL CHALLENGES RECORDED in challengeMatrixRows. NEGATIVE SEARCH PATHS: searched HRS Title 18 (Education — 302A, 302D) and HRS Title 9 (Public Property, Purchasing and Contracting — 103D, 103F) for a transportation-specific term cap, a special-education-specific procurement rule, an automatic evergreen-renewal authority, a board-approval requirement for school-bus contracts, and a separate special-education contracting framework — none located in any search path. Search platforms used: WebFetch (for direct PDF retrieval from data.capitol.hawaii.gov, spo.hawaii.gov, dod.hawaii.gov, and lrb.hawaii.gov), pdftotext for local text extraction (because Cloudflare blocks rich HTML retrieval from capitol.hawaii.gov and most Justia URLs return 403), and Web search via the Anthropic web tool for cross-checks on amendments and bill status. Firecrawl was unavailable during this validation run (token expired); WebFetch + pdftotext was a complete substitute because all primary sources are published as PDFs on official state domains.
sourcesReVerified: Governor's Proclamation Relating to School Bus Services (Aug. 17, 2023) — https://dod.hawaii.gov/hiema/files/2023/01/2023-08-17-Proclamation-Relating-to-School-Bus-Services.pdf
Hawaii LRB 2026 Passed-bills list (as of May 9, 2026) — https://lrb.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026_Passed.pdf
Hawaii State Procurement Office, HRS Reference page — https://spo.hawaii.gov/references/hrs/
sourcesConsulted:
- **HRS Chapter 103D (Hawaii Public Procurement Code)** — official Hawaii State Legislature PDF at https://data.capitol.hawaii.gov/sessions/session2017/HRS-Chapter-PDF's/HRS_0103D.pdf (sections 103D-102, 103D-104, 103D-301, 103D-302, 103D-306, 103D-307, 103D-309, 103D-315).
- **HRS Chapter 302A (Education)** — official Hawaii State Legislature PDF at https://data.capitol.hawaii.gov/sessions/session2017/HRS-Chapter-PDF's/HRS_0302A.pdf (sections 302A-406, 302A-407, 302A-407.5).
- **HAR Chapter 3-122 (Procurement of Goods, Services and Construction)** — Hawaii State Procurement Office official PDF at https://spo.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2013/11/3-122.pdf (sections 3-122-81, 3-122-149).
- **HAR section 3-122-3 (Extension of time on contracts), as amended July 10, 2025** — SPO official PDF at https://spo.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/RAM-HAR-3-122-Subchapters-2-to-5_am_PPB-revised-7-10-2025.pdf.
- **Act 257, Session Laws of Hawaii 2013 (S.B. 1082, Relating to Transportation of School Children)** — official PDF at https://data.capitol.hawaii.gov/sessions/sessionlaws/Years/SLH2013/SLH2013_Act257.pdf.
- **Governor's Proclamation Relating to School Bus Services (Josh Green, M.D., Aug. 17, 2023)** — Hawaii Emergency Management Agency PDF at https://dod.hawaii.gov/hiema/files/2023/01/2023-08-17-Proclamation-Relating-to-School-Bus-Services.pdf.
- **Hawaii Legislative Reference Bureau enacted-acts lists** for 2022, 2023, 2024, 2025 and 2026 passed-bills list — https://lrb.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2022_Acts.pdf, /2023_Acts.pdf, /2024_Acts.pdf, /2025_Acts.pdf, /2026_Passed.pdf.
- **Hawaii State Procurement Office, HRS Reference page** — https://spo.hawaii.gov/references/hrs/.
- **HIDOE Procurement and Contracts Branch / Vendors Working with HIDOE** — https://hawaiipublicschools.org/useful-links/vendors-working-with-hidoe/.
- **State of Hawaii Hawaii eProcurement (HePS) — sample HIDOE solicitation protest clause** — https://hands.ehawaii.gov/hands/api/opportunity-attachment?id=16769&attachmentId=23503 (corroborates HRS 103D / HAR 3-122/3-126 framework).
adversarialVerdictSummary: confirmed (6/6 fields); one Notes-section rule citation refuted-and-corrected (HAR 3-122-66.5 → HAR 3-122-81(d)); no substantive answer changed
